item.title: Barclays Says Robots May Offset 60% of China’s Population Slump
item.description: China’s deployment of humanoid robots could offset as much as 60% of its projected decline in the labor force by 2035, according to Barclays Plc, helping sustain the world’s largest industrial base as the population shrinks at a pace not seen in decades.

item.title: EQT CEO Says New Tech Fund Will Help Europe’s Scale-Up Problem
item.description: A new fund targeting Europe’s most promising startups will help the region create more global champions, according to the head of private equity group EQT AB.

item.title: New ASX CEO Must Drive Right Pace of Change, Outgoing Chief Says
item.description: ASX Ltd.’s incoming chief Anthony Attia will need to strike a balance between accelerating the exchange operator’s market infrastructure development while ensuring its stakeholders are not left behind, according to the firm’s outgoing leader.

item.title: Incoming CEO of Poland’s Zabka Seeks to Build Europe’s 7-Eleven
item.description: Poland’s leading convenience store chain, Zabka Group SA, will prioritize European expansion under its incoming chief executive officer.

item.title: Citi Says 5.5% May Be Next Key Level for 30-Year Treasury Yield
item.description: Bond traders are zeroing in on 5.5% as the new “round number” for 30-year US Treasury yields as inflation concerns ripple across markets, according to Citigroup Inc.

item.title: Bank of England Mulling Alternative to Stablecoin Holding Limits
item.description: The Bank of England is considering an alternative to its proposed limits on stablecoin holdings after a backlash from the industry.

item.title: Nickel Spikes After Report of Indonesian NPI Production Cut
item.description: Nickel prices spiked following a report of further output cuts in Indonesia, the top producer of the metal.

item.title: Fish Feed Firm BioMar Set For Biggest Copenhagen IPO Since 2018
item.description: Danish conglomerate Schouw & Co. set the price range for its initial public offering of fish feed producer BioMar on Tuesday, in what would be Copenhagen’s largest IPO since 2018.

item.title: Yango Targets 10 New African Markets in $150 Million Push
item.description: Ride-hailing operator Yango Group, plans to invest at least $150 million into African expansion this year, as the Dubai-based platform pushes into new markets.

item.title: India’s 360 ONE Seeks Up to $500 Million for Private Credit Fund
item.description: India’s 360 ONE Asset Management is seeking to raise as much as $500 million for its sixth private credit fund, according to people familiar with the transaction, underscoring sustained investor appetite in the country even as fundraising slows elsewhere.

item.title: Deutsche Bank’s Ozan Tarman and Aditya Singhal on Understanding the Macro Risks
item.description: A live conversation on keeping up with the news cycle.

item.title: Odd Lots: How Traders Keep Up With the News Cycle (Podcast)
item.description: It is hard to have a markets conversation that isn’t out of date within a minute or two. But we think this one, with Ozan Tarman and Aditya Singhal of Deutsche Bank, is basically evergreen. This conversation, recorded at our live show at Wilton’s Music Hall in London, is all about fundamentals: How Tarman, DB’s vice chair of global macro, and Singhal, the firm’s head of EM trading across rates, FX and Credit, make sense of conflicting headlines, whether the rally in tech stocks is to be believed

item.title: KKR to Dump Kokusai Electric Stake as AI Spurs Share Rally
item.description: Kokusai Electric Corp.’s biggest shareholder KKR & Co. is offloading its entire 10.6% stake about two and half years after the Japanese chip gear maker’s bumper initial public offering.

item.title: Global Inflation Weighs on G-7 as Higher Bond Yields Persist
item.description: Global finance chiefs are coming to terms with the new economic reality that a consumer-price shock they had hopes of skirting is looking likely to endure.

item.title: Ruble Tops Global Currencies as Putin Reaps Iran War Oil Gain
item.description: Russia’s ruble has muscled its way to become the world’s best performer against the dollar so far this quarter, buoyed by a surge in foreign currency receipts from oil sales following the outbreak of war in the Middle East.

item.title: Singapore Has Bought Enough LNG to Last for Rest of Year
item.description: Singapore has bought enough liquefied natural gas to last through the end of the year, as it replaces shipments stuck behind the Strait of Hormuz due to the Iran war.

item.title: Monzo’s Profit Jumps 44% as Fintech Moves Further Into Lending
item.description: Monzo Bank Ltd. reported a 44% jump in profit in the year through March, as growth in lending lifted interest income.

item.title: Fosun Picks Banks for Hong Kong IPO of Club Med Holiday Resort Chain
item.description: Fosun International Ltd. has picked banks for a planned Hong Kong initial public offering of holiday resort operator Club Med SAS, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: UK Energy Price Cap Seen Surging 13% in Biggest Gain Since 2023
item.description: The UK’s cap on household energy bills is set to jump 13% this summer as the war in Iran drives up wholesale gas and power costs, adding to broader inflationary pressures weighing on the economy.

item.title: Global Bond Selloff Threatens Turmoil in Weakest Asian Economies
item.description: Three of Asia’s most vulnerable economies are showing rising strains as their central banks come under pressure to tighten policy even as the economic hit from the Iran-war oil shock deepens.

item.title: Economic Outlook at Risk of Further Deterioration, OECD Says
item.description: Risks to the global economy have worsened as the conflict in the Middle East drags on, OECD Secretary General Mathias Cormann said on the sidelines of a Group of Seven meeting.

item.title: HK Hotel Firm Hit by Evergrande Loss Struggles to Refinance Loan
item.description: A Hong Kong hotel operator which suffered millions of dollars of losses on its investment in China Evergrande’s bonds is facing difficulty refinancing a HK$1.36 billion ($174 million) loan due next week, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Singapore Stocks Reclaim Record High on Haven Demand in Iran War
item.description: Singapore stocks climbed to a record as investors sought defensive havens during volatility sparked by the Iran war.

item.title: Thailand Defies Court Challenge to Push Through Crisis Borrowing
item.description: Thailand’s government is pushing ahead with a controversial 400 billion baht ($12 billion) emergency borrowing plan despite a court challenge that threatens to complicate its response to surging energy costs and slowing economic growth.

item.title: Kenya Cuts Diesel Prices After Deadly Protests Over Fuel Costs
item.description: Kenya cut diesel prices after deadly protests over surging fuel costs, seeking to ease pressure on transport operators and households as unrest over the rising cost of living intensifies.

item.title: Indonesian Markets Slump on Commodity Export Control Speculation
item.description: Indonesian markets fell as speculation mounted that the government will centralize commodity exports to control capital flows and shore up a plunging currency. Palm oil futures rose.

item.title: Nintendo Shares Rebound as AI Fatigue Fuels Japan Stock Rotation
item.description: Embattled Switch 2 maker Nintendo Co. enjoyed its biggest stock gain in two months on Tuesday as concerns about overvaluation in the AI sector sent investors on the hunt for bargains elsewhere.

item.title: Fujikura Shares Plunge After Three-Year Forecast Dents AI Hope
item.description: Fujikura Ltd. shares fell as much as 17% after its three-year forecast fell short of frothy expectations about artificial intelligence’s appetite for high-density optical-fiber networks.

item.title: Moonshot AI to Overhaul Structure to Win Nod for Hong Kong IPO
item.description: Moonshot AI has informed its investors it will revamp its corporate structure to pave the way for an initial public offering in Hong Kong, aligning with Beijing’s tightened requirements for Chinese companies seeking an overseas debut.

item.title: UK Job Cuts Accelerate as Iran War Casts Shadow on Outlook
item.description: UK employers slashed jobs by the most since the start of the pandemic in April, suggesting demand for workers is weakening as the Iran war pushes up energy costs and saps business confidence.
